best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback continuation remains the core edge.
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long leads PnL with 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, 100% wins, and worst MAE -403.2. It is tail-heavy but still the strongest repeatable contributor.

best_edges.edge: Charm-pin pullback is profitable and fast, but owns the largest tail.
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long produced 34 trades, $1237.7 PnL, 164.67 PnL/exposure hour, 5.3 minute median hold, and average MAE only -74.3, but worst MAE is -527.0.

best_edges.edge: 1DTE breakout is valuable fallback insurance when 0DTE is unavailable.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte has 20 trades, $1305 PnL, no slow trades, and 168.76 PnL/exposure hour. Prior 1DTE quality tightening hurt score and worsened worst MAE, so broad pruning should be avoided.

best_edges.action: protect but do not expand aggressively
best_edges.edge: Charm-wall breakaway is high value but low sample.
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long generated $985.6 from only 5 trades with 234.36 PnL/exposure hour. Worst MAE -346.8 and average MAE -148.96 make it worth monitoring, but sample size is too small for broad changes.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze is clean enough to keep stable.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long has 10 trades, $721 PnL, 187.16 PnL/exposure hour, and the best meaningful active worst MAE among repeat profiles at -208.25.

broken_edges.evidence: The six worst-MAE fingerprints are all still profitable winners, and good extended-VWAP winners contribute $674.9 with median MAE -14.875. A blunt extended-VWAP or structure gate risks blocking the edge.
broken_edges.problem: Tail signatures overlap heavily with protected winners.

broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE local quality gates reduced net profit by $317-$558 and worsened worst MAE from -527 to -603.75 in tested variants.
broken_edges.problem: Quality tightening removed useful 1DTE insurance without reducing tail risk.

broken_edges.evidence: Fallback-prune-pullback-retain was flat-to-negative, losing $23.4 and score while leaving worst MAE unchanged. Stale/expired samples total only 6 trades.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback samples are too small and prior tightening did not solve the tail.

broken_edges.evidence: Call-wall expansion experiments lost $305-$482 and worsened worst MAE to as low as -638.75, despite the single clean current trade.
broken_edges.problem: One-trade call-wall evidence did not survive sample expansion.

broken_edges.evidence: These active router profiles have zero trades in the latest run and no evidence of contribution.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable in this sample; optimizing them now adds noise.

market_regime_read: The current leader is a favorable long-only momentum/pullback sample: 107 trades, $7125.8 PnL, 100% realized win rate, 0.4% drawdown, no slow >=12h trades, and 65 fast under-15m trades. The real problem is hidden heat: average MAE -101.67, worst MAE -527, and only 9.3% of trades reach MFE before MAE. Because all worst tails still recovered profitably, the next risk-reducer work should be surgical: reduce deep underwater holds without blocking fast winners or the 1DTE fallback edge.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate deep-heat pullback state from normal pullback continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Existing config gates cannot reliably distinguish profitable weak-structure pullbacks from deep-heat rows without blocking too much PnL.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-state ES structure, VWAP distance/slope, early path diagnostics, option data state, and profile-level attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback entries with weak/mixed structure, delayed early progress, and low path MFE before MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% of pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and average MAE from -110.16.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Route 1DTE pullback/reclaim states separately from 1DTE breakout.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Prior breakout_1dte tightening harmed the fallback edge; the issue may be regime mismatch rather than weak quality.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries, ES reclaim/pullback state, 1DTE option map state, and breakout-vs-pullback reason codes.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1305 with improved 1DTE average MAE versus -123.86 and no total score regression.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Classify extended-VWAP rejection into recoverable fast winners versus slow heat.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Current legal thresholds nearly catch bad rows but protected extended-VWAP winners overlap the same state.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Extended VWAP Tail-State Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Normalized ES-VWAP distance, rolling-high distance, pullback-from-high, RSI/ADX slope, profile, and first-15m path behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Entries above VWAP with rejection warnings, low early MFE, or no positive-before-MAE behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -403 while total PnL retained >=97% and fast winner count remains within -3 of baseline.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Test dormant 1DTE pullback only as a router split, not as broad 1DTE pruning.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May be unreachable or may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades if router precedence is too broad.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant pullback_long_1dte Activation Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router state for VALID_1DTE_ONLY, existing 1DTE profile match reasons, and post-activation trade attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY state where pullback/reclaim criteria are present and breakout quality is not decisive.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 attributed trades, positive profile PnL, total PnL >=98% of leader, and 1DTE average MAE improves.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection boundary that narrowly missed the worst charm-pin row and avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance or structure-score tightening. Treat protected extended-VWAP winners as a hard constraint.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce worst MAE from -527 without blocking the extended-VWAP winner bucket.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin tail was a threshold miss, not a failure of the whole edge; small boundary nudges may catch it while preserving fast winners.
next_experiments.name: Surgical Extended-VWAP Threshold Nudge
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total PnL >=97%, fast_under_15m trades down <=3, and completed trades down <=5.
next_experiments.risk: A too-wide nudge will block profitable extended-VWAP recoveries and reduce score.

next_experiments.config_bias: Focus on the charm-pin tail-risk box that previously improved objective, but test a narrower validation around the worst trade's high VWAP distance, large pullback/rolling-high distance, weak RSI slope, and low ADX. Avoid setup-quality-only floors.
next_experiments.goal: Remove or resize the -527 charm-pin tail while preserving most charm-pin throughput.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The accepted charm-pin tail box family is the only proven risk reducer so far; a refined box can catch the remaining worst tail more cleanly than global filters.
next_experiments.name: Charm-Pin Tail Box Refinement v2
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE <= -403, profile PnL retained >=90%, and total PnL retained >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: The worst trade's setup score is close to many winners, so setup floors alone would over-prune.

next_experiments.config_bias: Explore a profile-scoped consequence for high entry-veto points only when combined with no early-quality confirmation proxies already available at entry. Do not globally block all 4-5 point entries because those buckets are profitable.
next_experiments.goal: Test whether clustered warning reasons can reduce long underwater holds without killing high-veto profitable buckets.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Deep tails often entered with multiple warnings, but entry-veto point buckets also contain strong winners; only profile/context-scoped escalation is worth testing.
next_experiments.name: Contextual Veto Consequence Test
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE and average MAE improve with total PnL >=98% and no loss of more than 3 fast winners.
next_experiments.risk: Entry-veto points alone are not separative; broad escalation would block profitable trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run a narrow pullback weak-structure holdout focused on delayed-to-profit and weak ES structure states, not a blanket structure-score raise. Prior evidence shows structure score 3 and 4 buckets are profitable.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce pullback_long average MAE and worst MAE while keeping its top-PnL role intact.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Pullback_long is the largest edge but has -110 average MAE; a narrow weak-structure/deceleration slice may improve risk-adjusted quality.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Weak-Structure Tail Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long PnL retained >=92%, average MAE improves, worst MAE improves from -403.2, and PnL/exposure hour stays >=240.
next_experiments.risk: Weak-structure pullbacks are also a major profit source, so over-tightening will damage the core edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not tune breakout_long_1dte with stricter breakout quality. If testing 1DTE risk, use a router/profile split into a pullback companion holdout and measure cannibalization explicitly.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE MAE by separating regimes instead of pruning the profitable fallback profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: 1DTE tails may come from regime mismatch; a pullback companion can improve attribution while preserving fallback PnL.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Router Split Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1305, 1DTE average MAE better than -123.86, and total score not lower than leader.
next_experiments.risk: Router activation may steal trades from breakout_long_1dte without enough risk improvement.

research_notes: The leaderboard is flat at the top and prior accepted work came from runtime-risk/charm-pin-tail families. The next optimizer should not chase raw PnL or activate dormant concepts broadly. The strongest evidence is that worst-MAE rows are threshold near-misses inside a profitable edge, while broad quality/fallback/call-wall expansions have already failed. Treat all experiments as small, reversible risk-reducer probes with protected winner fingerprints as constraints.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment that reduces total net profit below 98% of the $7125.8 leader unless worst MAE improves materially beyond -403.
Reject any broad gate that removes more than 5 completed trades or more than 3 fast_under_15m trades without a clear MAE improvement.
Do not use setup_quality_score floors alone; the worst tail at 61.99 is too close to profitable buckets and a >=62 floor would retain only 45% PnL.
Do not repeat 1DTE breakout quality tightening or call-wall expansion unless isolated as a new-profile/router holdout.
Keep no-0DTE fallback changes frozen until a larger stale/missing sample exists.
